description:
# Final consolidation; removes duplicate single-pattern lowering

## What changes

`lower_pattern_bind` (src/ir_lower.rs ~1907) becomes a thin wrapper:

```rust
fn lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block) {
    let matrix = Matrix {
        subjects: vec![subject],
        rows: vec![Row {
            patterns: vec![pat.clone()],
            guard: None,
            body_id: BodyId::noop(),
        }],
    };
    lower_pattern_matrix(ctx, matrix, fail_block, |_, _| Ok(()))
}
```

Used by: assignment positions in lower_case, lower_with's `<-` step,
multi-head match (line 877, 1218, 1682), and comprehension generators.
After this ticket they all flow through the same path.

## What stays

- The function signature `lower_pattern_bind(ctx, subject, pat, fail_block)`
  — call sites unchanged.
- All single-pattern semantics.

## What's removed

- The 230-line body of `lower_pattern_bind` (Pattern::Tuple, ::List,
  ::Map, ::Bitstring, ::As, etc.) — subsumed by lower_pattern_matrix.
- The standalone `Pattern::Tuple` `Prim::TypeTest` emission (line 1955) —
  the matrix emits this when needed.
- The `next: BlockId` threading through recursion in lower_pattern_bind.

## Acceptance

- Single-pattern fixtures (let, comprehension, multi-head match):
  byte-identical OR strictly smaller goldens.
- Pattern::Bitstring still works (matrix's bitstring fallback path).
- All three execution paths.
- Net LOC reduction in ir_lower.rs (verify; should be ~200 lines down).

## Risk

Bitstring patterns are the most special-cased — verify the matrix's
bitstring fallback produces semantically identical IR to today's
lower_pattern_bind branch.

## Why last

The matrix infrastructure needs to be battle-tested on
multi-clause/case/with first. Once those work and produce correct +
tight goldens, the 1-row case is trivial. Bonus: at this point the
matrix has handled enough patterns that the bitstring/map fallback
path is well-exercised.
title: fz-ul4.43.H: lower_pattern_bind becomes 1-row matrix wrapper
